  discuss approach — a rejected large PR is disappointing for
  everyone.
-->

  - Why is the change needed?

  Notable implementation decisions, tricky bits, edge cases.
  Skip if the diff speaks for itself.

## Test plan

- [ ] Unit tests pass: `./manage.py test` or `pytest tests/`
- [ ] Manual verification (list the steps you ran):
  <!--
    1. ...
    2. ...
    3. ...
  -->

## Checklist

- [ ] Every commit is signed off with `git commit -s` — see
      [DCO in CONTRIBUTING.md](../blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-certificate-of-origin-dco).
- [ ] Tests added or updated for new / changed behaviour.
- [ ] Docs updated if user-visible behaviour changed
      (README, ARCHITECTURE, CLAUDE.md, admin help content).
- [ ] No new inline `<style>` blocks or `style=""` attributes; no
      inline `<script>` or `onclick`-style handlers (CSP-safe).
- [ ] No hardcoded absolute paths (`/mnt/...`, `/home/...`), IPs, or
      environment-specific hostnames.
- [ ] No accidental secrets: `.env` files, real API keys, real
      licence data.

## Screenshots (UI changes only)

<!--
  Before / after images, or a short screen recording.
  Especially valuable for admin theme, storefront rendering, or
  page-builder changes.
-->
